column2: A network cable will run near equipment that creates electrical interference. Which twisted-pair cable type is the better fit: UTP or STP?
column4: STP, because shielded twisted-pair cable adds shielding to reduce interference.
column5:
Exam Trap

UTP is common for ordinary Ethernet runs, but the question adds an interference concern. STP is the better match when shielding is the reason for choosing the cable.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.23; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2
column10: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Basic HighYield TwistedPair CableTypes Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which cable feature should a technician look for when an Ethernet cable must be installed underground between nearby structures?
column5:
Why

Direct-burial cable is built for underground outdoor exposure. In the 3.2 scope, it is tied to shielded twisted-pair cabling and protection from the environment.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.23; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2
column10: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Basic DirectBurial TwistedPair Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: A cable will be installed in an air-handling space above a drop ceiling. What cable rating should the technician verify?
column5:
Why

Plenum-rated cable is about the jacket and installation environment. The point is not higher network speed; it is using cable suitable for spaces that can move air through a building.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.23; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2
column10: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Basic HighYield Plenum CableTypes Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which cable and connector pairing is commonly associated with cable TV or cable modem service?
column5:
Exam Trap

RJ45 is the common Ethernet modular connector. F-type is the threaded connector associated with coaxial cable service.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 pp.23, 28; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2
column10: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Basic Coax FType CableTypes Source::Messer-v170

column2: For a long-distance fiber link, should a technician generally expect single-mode or multimode fiber?
column5:
Why

Single-mode fiber is the long-distance fiber option in this objective. Multimode fiber is associated with shorter runs and less expensive light sources.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.24; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2
column10: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Basic HighYield Fiber CableTypes Scenario Source::Messer-v170

column2: Why is it unsafe to assume that every USB-C port supports the same features?
column4: USB-C describes the connector shape, not one guaranteed protocol or capability.
column5:
Exam Trap

A USB-C connector can be used for USB data, power, Thunderbolt, or video features depending on the device and cable. The connector shape alone does not prove what signals are supported.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 pp.25-26, 28; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2
column10: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Basic HighYield USBC PeripheralCables ExamTrap Source::Messer-v170

column2: Which high-speed peripheral connection can use a USB-C connector while carrying data and power on the same cable?
column5:
Exam Trap

Newer Thunderbolt connections can use the USB-C connector, but USB-C and Thunderbolt are not the same thing. USB-C is the physical connector; Thunderbolt is the high-speed interface.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.25; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2

column2: A technician needs text-based console access to older equipment through an RS-232-style connection. Which connector type is commonly associated with that serial connection?
column5:
Real Help Desk

Serial console access still appears on some infrastructure devices. A modern laptop may need a USB-to-serial adapter to connect to that management port.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 pp.25, 28; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2

column2: Which two video cable families in Objective 3.2 are digital connections commonly used for audio/video or monitor output?
column5:
Why

HDMI is common for digital audio/video devices. DisplayPort is common for PC display connections and can also carry digital audio/video data.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.26; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2

column2: How should a technician distinguish VGA from DVI at a high level?
column4: VGA is analog video only; DVI is a video connector family that can carry digital, analog, or both depending on the DVI variant.
column5:
Exam Trap

Do not treat all DVI connectors as identical. DVI-A, DVI-D, and DVI-I differ by signal support, while VGA remains analog video only.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.26; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2
column10: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Basic DVI VGA VideoCables Source::Messer-v170

column2: What is the main purpose difference between SATA and eSATA cabling?
column4: SATA is used for internal drive connections; eSATA is used for external SATA device connections.
column5:
Source Note

Objective 3.2 covers SATA and eSATA as hard drive cables. Detailed storage-device behavior belongs in Objective 3.4.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes

column2: When does a cable connection need a converter instead of a simple adapter?
column4: Use a converter when the signal format must change, not just the physical connector.
column5:
Exam Trap

An adapter is enough when the signals are electrically compatible and only the connector shape needs to change. A converter is needed when the source and destination use different signal formats.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.27; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2

column2: Which modular connector is commonly used for Ethernet, and which is commonly associated with telephone or DSL service?
column4: RJ45 is used for Ethernet; RJ11 is associated with telephone or DSL service.
column5:
Exam Trap

RJ45 and RJ11 look related because both are modular connectors, but RJ45 is larger and tied to Ethernet in this objective.
column8: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column9: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.28; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2

column2: What is the purpose of a punchdown block in structured cabling?
column4: It provides a place to terminate and connect individual network wires.
column5:
Why

A punchdown block is not a plug like RJ45. Wires are pressed into the block so cabling can be terminated and cross-connected in a structured cabling area.
